canonicalProductName.note: CONFIRM with the product owner before MFP-21/MFP-22. Today the brand appears three ways: 'SynapseIDE' (App.tsx:215,392,903), 'Synapse IDE' (App.tsx:1249), 'Urban Analytics Workbench' (document.title App.tsx:1309; package.json name 'urban-analytics-workbench'). index.html still says 'Coder App - Professional Code Editor'.

prompts.title: Fix the undefined GOLD runtime bug in KeysModal
prompts.problem: KeysModal.tsx:174 renders style={{ color:GOLD }}, but GOLD is never declared or imported anywhere in src/ (repo-wide grep finds only GOLDEN_RATIO in constants/design.ts:465 and GOLDEN_PAIRS in a test). This is a 'Cannot find name GOLD' typecheck failure and a render-time ReferenceError.
steps: Grep repo-wide for `\bGOLD\b` to confirm there is no intended import to restore (only GOLDEN_* exist).
Replace `color:GOLD` on line 174 with a CSS-variable amber token consistent with the file's existing var(--syn-*) usage.
constraints: Keep the title node's existing inline-style object shape.
Do NOT touch z-index here — that is centralized in MFP-15.
acceptance: npm run typecheck passes with no 'Cannot find name GOLD'.
KeysModal mounts without a ReferenceError.
Title renders in the amber accent.

prompts.title: Promote useFocusTrap to a shared hook
prompts.problem: The correct, reusable trap (re-queries focusables per Tab; filters hidden/aria-hidden/negative-tabindex; captures+restores opener with a document.contains guard; wraps both directions; data-map-skip-link aware activate()) lives under map/, so non-map modals cannot find it and reimplement it. 6+ trap copies exist.
steps: Move the file contents verbatim to src/hooks/useFocusTrap.ts (keep all named exports: FOCUSABLE_SELECTOR, getFocusableElements, useFocusTrap).
Replace the old map path with `export * from '@/hooks/useFocusTrap';` so map imports do not change.
acceptance: src/centerpanel/components/map/__tests__/map-accessibility.test.ts passes unchanged.
useMapExplorerLifecycle.ts still resolves the hook.

prompts.title: Add a ref-counted useScrollLock hook
prompts.problem: Modal.tsx:178-186 mutates document.body.style.overflow directly. With stacked modals, the first to close restores scroll while another is still open (M5).
steps: Implement useScrollLock(active: boolean) backed by a module-level counter.
On first lock: save the prior overflow value, set 'hidden'. Release only when the counter returns to 0, restoring the saved value.
acceptance: Unit test: two locks then release one keeps overflow:hidden; releasing the last restores the original value.

prompts.title: Add useInertBackground hook
prompts.problem: Only the Map Explorer shell inerts the background; the base Modal and most bespoke modals leave the app reachable by AT (M6).
steps: Implement useInertBackground(active) that, while active, sets inert (with aria-hidden='true' fallback) on the app root's siblings, excluding the modal/portal root and known overlay roots — generalize the exclusion logic in MapWorkspaceShell.tsx:594-599.
Restore (remove inert/aria-hidden) on deactivate.
constraints: Never inert the portal/modal root itself.
acceptance: Unit test: while active a background button is inert/not tabbable; after deactivate the attributes are gone.

prompts.title: Publish the full z-index scale as CSS variables
prompts.problem: The scale (backdrop:1040, statusBar:9999, modal:10050, popover:10060, tooltip:10070, toast:10080) is defined in design.ts but only --z-modal is published as a CSS var, so bespoke modals hardcode raw numbers (§4.3).
steps: Publish --z-backdrop, --z-popover, --z-tooltip, --z-toast from DESIGN_TOKENS.zIndex alongside the existing --z-modal.
Add a --z-system-banner tier above toast for the global gold bars (replaces the 2147483648 literals in MFP-15).
Do NOT change consumers here — MFP-15 does that.
constraints: Single source of truth = design.ts zIndex.
Keep numeric values identical to design.ts.
acceptance: All documented tiers reachable as CSS vars.
npm run color:guard clean.

prompts.title: Rebuild the base Modal on the shared foundation
prompts.problem: Base Modal has no focus trap (L189-199), no focus restore, hardcoded id='modal-title' (L214,217), global unscoped Escape/click (L162-168), direct body scroll mutation (L178-186), no inert, no live region, palette min-width:640px floor (L101), token bypass #121212 (L65).
steps: Consume useFocusTrap(isOpen); attach trapRef to the panel (ModalContainer) → trap + restore (M1/M2).
Replace the body-scroll effect with useScrollLock(isOpen && preventBodyScroll) (M5).
Add useInertBackground(isOpen) (M6).
Replace hardcoded id='modal-title' with a useId()-derived id (M3).
Scope Escape to the dialog (keydown on overlay/panel) instead of global useKeyPress; keep closeOnEscape/closeOnOverlayClick props (M4).
Add a polite aria-live announcement on open (M7).
Add optional ariaLabel + describedby props; render aria-labelledby when titled else aria-label. Keep the public API backward-compatible.
Source the palette surface from SYNAPSE_OVERLAY.surface (M8); parametrize/lower the min-width:640px floor so the dialog can reflow (M9).
constraints: Backward-compatible props (Command Palette, Global Search, New Project, Settings must not break).
exactOptionalPropertyTypes: pass undefined explicitly, never optional-vs-undefined mismatch.
acceptance: Command Palette, Global Search, New Project, Settings gain trap+restore+inert with no per-consumer change.
Two simultaneous Modals no longer emit duplicate modal-title ids.
Map Explorer e2e a11y stays green.

prompts.title: Foundation unit tests (Modal + hooks)
prompts.problem: The foundation that most dialogs depend on is untested (§8).
steps: Add a reusable assertDialogA11y(render) helper: initial focus moves in; Tab/Shift+Tab wrap; focus restores to opener on close; Escape closes.
Add scroll-lock ref-counting, background inert toggling, and unique-title-ids-across-two-instances assertions.
Assert ariaLabel/describedby wiring.
acceptance: New tests pass; coverage policy satisfied.

prompts.title: Make AiSettingsModal a conformant dialog
prompts.problem: Unnamed dialog (L159-166), not portaled (L158), no Escape, no focus trap/restore, close button '×' has only title (L167), z-index:1000 below backdrop/statusbar (module.css:11), pervasive any (L10,82-85,126-128).
steps: Portal the dialog to document.body (createPortal).
Add a heading id and aria-labelledby='ai-settings-title' (AS3).
Consume useFocusTrap for trap+restore; add Escape-to-close on the dialog root (AS1/AS2).
Give the close button aria-label='Close AI settings' (AS5).
Set the overlay z-index to var(--z-modal) (AS6).
Replace Record<string, any>/keyPayload:any with typed shapes from useAiConfigStore.types (AS7).
prompts.codeSketch:
return createPortal(
{ if(e.key==='Escape') onCancel(); }}>
AI Settings
acceptance: axe: no serious violations.
Dialog has an accessible name.
Tab cycles within; Escape closes; focus returns to opener.
typecheck clean with no any.

prompts.title: Make MapServiceDialog keyboard-conformant + fix roles/shadowing
prompts.problem: aria-modal='true' (L789) with no trap, no Escape, no initial focus, no restore; role='listbox' with <button> children (L599-619); boundsLabel is both a prop and a module function (L254,290).
steps: Wrap the dialog body in MapDialogShell (provides trap+Escape+restore), or consume useFocusTrap directly.
Fix the layer list: role='listbox' + child role='option' (with aria-selected), or drop the listbox role and use a labelled group of buttons.
Rename the module-level boundsLabel function to remove the prop/function collision.
Route fetch failures through reportError (sets up the MFP-19 pattern).
constraints: centerpanel = no Tailwind; CSS Modules only.
reportError not raw toast for errors.
acceptance: Keyboard users Tab within and Escape out.
SR announces options.
map-* e2e specs pass.

prompts.title: NewFileModal: correct dialog role, keyboard selectors, focus, data extraction
prompts.problem: role='dialog' on the click-to-close overlay (L783-788) not the panel (L790); category/language/template are clickable <div>s with no role/tabindex/keydown (mouse-only); no focus trap/restore; dead inline &:hover/&:focus pseudo-selectors (L585,632,720); ~470 lines of static data inlined (L17-388).
steps: Move role='dialog'/aria-modal/aria-label onto the panel (NF1).
Convert each selection group to role='radiogroup' of keyboard-operable role='radio' (or real <button>): tabIndex, Enter/Space, aria-checked, accessible labels (NF2).
Add focus trap+restore (useFocusTrap, or render via the rebuilt base Modal) (NF3).
Extract getTemplateContentByLanguage, LANGUAGE_CATEGORIES, TEMPLATE_TYPES to src/components/file-explorer/newFileTemplates.ts and lazy-load (NF4).
Remove dead pseudo-selectors; move hover/focus styling to CSS Module/styled (NF5).
prompts.codeSketch: {templates.map(t => ( ))}
constraints: Lazy import keeps the chunk budget.
acceptance: category→language→template→filename flow operable by keyboard alone.
axe clean.
SR boundary is the panel.
NewFileModal.tsx shrinks materially.

prompts.title: NewProjectModal: keyboard-operable template cards
prompts.problem: TemplateCard is a clickable <div onClick> (L161-172) with no role/tabIndex/key handler/aria-checked and no AT description.
steps: Convert the template grid to role='radiogroup' (aria-label='Project template').
Make each card a keyboard-operable role='radio' (Enter/Space, aria-checked, aria-describedby → its description text).
Keep the existing visual styling.
constraints: Built on base Modal (gaps fixed by MFP-06).
acceptance: Template selection works by keyboard.
axe clean.

prompts.title: GlobalSearch: combobox/listbox semantics, arrow nav, reflow
prompts.problem: role='listbox' with <button> children + aria-selected (L428,450) is invalid (GS2); incomplete tab pattern (role='tab' with no tabpanel/aria-controls/roving, L413-424) — really filter toggles (GS3); arrow nav dead-ends once a row is focused, no Home/End/wrap (L374-390) (GS4); palette min-width:640px overflows <688px (GS5).
steps: Adopt a combobox+listbox pattern: input role='combobox' with aria-expanded/aria-controls/aria-activedescendant; results role='listbox' with children role='option' (not buttons); select via aria-activedescendant rather than moving DOM focus into rows (fixes GS4).
Replace the scope role='tab' set with a segmented control / role='radiogroup' (or complete the tab pattern with aria-controls); add roving + Home/End (GS3).
Make the panel responsive (lift the 640px floor via MFP-06's parametrized size, or use a non-palette size); optionally wrap input in role='search' (GS5).
constraints: Keep file/content/artifact grouping aligned with flatResults index.
acceptance: axe clean.
Keyboard nav works end-to-end (arrows/Home/End/Enter/Escape).
Usable at 320px.

prompts.title: Migrate bespoke focus traps onto the shared hook
prompts.problem: Five hand-rolled traps duplicate useFocusTrap. WelcomeModal copies the selector/query helpers (L60-82). UrbanAnalyticsModal binds its trap listener to the modal element, not document (L805), so it cannot recapture escaped focus (UA1).
steps: Replace each hand-rolled trap with useFocusTrap.
Delete WelcomeModal's FOCUSABLE_MODAL_SELECTOR/getFocusableModalElements.
For UrbanAnalyticsModal, the hook's document-level capture fixes UA1.
Preserve each modal's focus-restore semantics (the hook already restores).
acceptance: All five keep trap+restore.
One audited trap path remains.
Bundle does not grow.

prompts.title: Standardize accessible names across all dialogs
prompts.problem: Inconsistent naming — WelcomeModal uses aria-label while a visible <h1 id='welcome-modal-title'> (L708) is never referenced; MapDialogShell uses aria-label only — its <h2> (L338) is unwired, so all 7 shell dialogs lack a programmatic title.
steps: WelcomeModal: set aria-labelledby='welcome-modal-title' and drop the duplicate aria-label.
MapDialogShell: generate a title id, render it on the <h2>, set aria-labelledby on the dialog — propagating a real name to all shell dialogs.
Verify every role='dialog' in the repo has aria-labelledby (preferred) or aria-label (AiSettings handled by MFP-08).
acceptance: axe 'dialog has accessible name' passes for every modal.
Names point at visible headings where present.

prompts.title: Apply z-index tokens to bespoke modals (fix occlusion)
prompts.problem: Five z-index strategies; concrete occlusion bugs — AI Settings 1000 (below backdrop/statusbar), Keys 2200, Unsaved 9999 (statusbar tier, below modal). Gold bars use 2147483648 (UrbanAnalyticsModal:1291, WelcomeModal:945, EnhancedIDE:1696=999999).
steps: Replace modal overlay z-indexes with var(--z-modal): Keys (2200), AiSettings (1000), Unsaved (9999).
Replace global gold-bar values with var(--z-system-banner) (from MFP-05).
Leave UrbanAnalyticsModal's deliberate 10049 dialog z-index unless it now conflicts.
constraints: No raw z-index literals left in modal overlays.
acceptance: No modal can be occluded by the status bar/backdrop.
color:guard / screenshot spot-check unchanged.

prompts.title: SettingsModal: extract logic, fix anti-patterns, enable reflow
prompts.problem: ~19 useState; render-injected inline <style> (L690-728); hand-rolled virtualization reading scrollTop during render (L829-894); network key verification embedded (L613-631); brittle globalThis.__KEY_ROUTER_SUSPENDED__ (L188-201,563-564); ref-in-deps anti-patterns (L448,573); fixed height:560px + base min-width:640px reflow floor (L17-21).
steps: Extract key-verification into src/services/ai/verifyProviderKey.ts with unit tests; the component only calls it.
Replace hand-rolled virtualization with a tested useVirtualList hook (or vetted lib in budget) — no scrollTop reads during render.
Remove globalThis.__KEY_ROUTER_SUSPENDED__ in favor of a scoped keydown handler.
Fix ref-in-deps effects (L448,573).
Lift the 560px/640px floor so the modal reflows (coordinate with MFP-06).
acceptance: Component shrinks; verification + virtualization have unit tests; usable at 320px; ARIA unchanged.

prompts.title: Urban Analytics + Welcome: reduced-motion, typed events, cleanups
prompts.problem: Close setTimeouts ignore prefers-reduced-motion (WelcomeModal:466, UrbanAnalyticsModal:521); UrbanAnalyticsModal dispatches untyped window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('synapse:…')) (L687-756) instead of synapseBus; file-wide jsx-a11y/no-noninteractive-element-interactions suppression (L1); leftover aria-hidden={false} on .midCol (L966).
steps: Gate both close-delay setTimeouts on prefers-reduced-motion (skip the delay when reduced).
Replace CustomEvent dispatches with typed synapseBus.emit(...); add any missing event to SynapseBusEventMap (IDs/refs only, no heavy payloads).
Correct the dialog root's interaction handlers so the file-wide a11y-lint suppression can be removed (or narrow it with justification).
Delete the no-op aria-hidden={false}.
acceptance: Reduced-motion users get instant close; no untyped cross-module events; lint suppression removed/narrowed.

prompts.title: Map dialog family: dead code, responsive grids, memoization
prompts.problem: MapStartDialog: unused dialogRef (L176), unused onAddDemoPack (L42), dead demoBadge branch (L404), risk of double aria-modal when wrapped. MapExportDialog grid minmax(360px,…) minmax(320px,…)=680px min (L31) and MapColumnarImportDialog 1.15fr/0.85fr (L54) don't stack. MapCsvImportDialog profiles unmemoized every render (L131-134). MapWorkspaceShell getPanelRailStyle ignores the height param.
steps: Remove dead ref/prop/branch in MapStartDialog; ensure it doesn't set aria-modal when wrapped.
Make the two grids responsive (auto-fit/stacking under a breakpoint).
Wrap CSV profiling in useMemo keyed on its inputs.
Use or remove the ignored height param.
constraints: centerpanel = no Tailwind, CSS Modules only.
acceptance: npm run deadcode shows no new dead exports.
Both dialogs usable at 320px.
CSV dialog no longer reprofiles every render.

prompts.title: Decompose MapExplorerModalRuntimeCore (staged)
prompts.problem: God-component (~6791 lines, ~325 hooks); ~170-field View props spread in one JSX element (View:37-208; Core:6609); three pure passthrough files; 51 direct toast* calls, 0 reportError (Core:137); no top-level shell error boundary; eager analysis-service web + SAMPLE_BUILDINGS + SpatialDB (Core:21,46-48,112).
steps: STAGE A: collapse the three passthrough files into one (each its own PR).
STAGE B: replace the ~170-prop View interface with grouped context objects / per-panel context providers; continue lifting panel state into existing controller hooks.
STAGE C: route the 51 toast* error calls through reportError (keep success/info toasts).
STAGE D: add a top-level shell error boundary (in addition to per-panel MapPanelErrorBoundary).
STAGE E: lazy-load the eager analysis-service web where not needed on open; verify with perf:budgets.
constraints: Do not bundle with foundation changes.
Each stage = its own PR behind green tests.
acceptance: Core shrinks materially; View prop surface grouped; errors go through reportError; modal open time improves; a11y/e2e suites green.

prompts.title: Test coverage + regression guardrails for all modals
prompts.problem: Modal-a11y tests exist only for Map Explorer. Untested: base Modal, GlobalSearch, KeysModal, SettingsModal, AiSettingsModal, WelcomeModal, UrbanAnalyticsModal, UnsavedChangesDialog, MapServiceDialog, MapStartDialog (§8).
steps: Add unit tests using the assertDialogA11y helper (MFP-07) for each untested modal: role/name, initial focus, Tab/Shift+Tab wrap, focus restore, Escape.

Extend e2e/accessibility-audit.spec.ts with a parametrized loop that opens each app modal, runs axe (no serious/critical), verifies scoped Escape returns focus to the trigger, and checks reflow at 320px.

Add CI guardrails: an ESLint rule (or color:guard-style check) forbidding raw z-index literals in modal files, and a check that any role='dialog' has an accessible name. Keep lint:no-tailwind-centerpanel green.
acceptance: Every modal has ≥1 focus/Escape/axe test; guardrails fail on a regression; npm run validate:rc passes.

prompts.title: Branding & product-name consistency across modals and shell
prompts.problem: The product name appears three ways: 'SynapseIDE' (App.tsx:215,392,903), 'Synapse IDE' (App.tsx:1249), 'Urban Analytics Workbench' (document.title App.tsx:1309). Modal titles/copy are inconsistent. There is no single brand source of truth.
steps: CONFIRM the canonical name with the product owner (proposed: full 'Synapse IDE — Urban Analytics Workbench', short 'Synapse IDE').
Create src/constants/brand.ts exporting BRAND = { full, short, tagline, version } as the single source of truth.
Replace hardcoded name strings in App.tsx and modal titles (Welcome heading L708, Urban modal title, Settings/Command Palette titles) with BRAND constants.
Ensure document.title (App.tsx:1309) uses BRAND.full.
prompts.branding: Use the welcome/about/empty-state surfaces as the primary branding moments. Keep the minimal-premium design language (dense typography, thin separators, restrained amber accent) — no marketing hero layouts.
constraints: Do not invent a tagline without owner sign-off.
acceptance: One brand source of truth; no divergent name strings in modals/shell; document.title consistent.

prompts.title: Release/publish assets: index.html, favicon, meta, version
prompts.problem: index.html <title> still says 'Coder App - Professional Code Editor' (L7) and favicon is the default Vite logo /vite.svg (L5). No meta description, og:/twitter cards, or theme-color. document.title is set at runtime (App.tsx:1309) but the static title is wrong (bad SEO/share preview/tab label).
steps: Set <title> to BRAND.full; add <meta name='description'>, og:title/og:description/og:image, twitter:card, and <meta name='theme-color'>.
Add a real product favicon (public/favicon.svg) and reference it; remove the /vite.svg default.
Align package.json name with BRAND (kebab) and bump version for the release (confirm semver with owner).
Confirm App.tsx:1309 runtime title matches the static title.
prompts.branding: This is the public-facing first impression (browser tab, social share, PWA). Keep it consistent with MFP-21's BRAND constants.
acceptance: Tab title and share preview show the correct brand; favicon is the product mark; npm run build succeeds; e2e:a11y green.
